title: limonium acutifolium subsp. bosanum
intro: Limonium acutifolium subsp. bosanum, also known as Bosman's sea lavender, is a plant species belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving skin conditions, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal properties. Limonium acutifolium subsp. bosanum is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
